Query, analyze, and stream data into Goo: Query, analyze, and stream data into Google BigQuery — the fully managed, serverless data warehouse
Execute a SQL query on BigQuery and retu: Execute a SQL query on BigQuery and return the results as flat rows
Creates a single new row in a BigQuery t: Creates a single new row in a BigQuery table. Column fields are loaded from the table schema.
Creates new rows of data in a BigQuery t: Creates new rows of data in a BigQuery table (accepts an array of row objects). Rows are available to query within seconds.
Deletes one or more rows from a BigQuery: Deletes one or more rows from a BigQuery table using a SQL WHERE condition.
Updates one or more existing rows in a B: Updates one or more existing rows in a BigQuery table using SQL SET and WHERE expressions.
Find a single row by specifying a WHERE: Find a single row by specifying a WHERE clause and an optional ORDER BY. Returns the first matching row, or empty if none found.
Searches for a row matching a WHERE clau: Searches for a row matching a WHERE clause. If found, returns it. If not found, inserts the provided row data and returns that.
Retrieves the result rows from a complet: Retrieves the result rows from a completed BigQuery query job by Job ID. Use this after a "Query Job Completed" trigger to fetch the full result set.
Imports a batch of rows into a BigQuery: Imports a batch of rows into a BigQuery table. Accepts a JSON array or newline-delimited JSON (NDJSON). Large datasets are automatically split into chunks.
Make a custom API call to a specific end: Make a custom API call to a specific endpoint
The SQL query to run. Uses standard SQL: The SQL query to run. Uses standard SQL syntax. Example: `SELECT * FROM \`my_dataset.my_table\` LIMIT 100`
Maximum number of rows to return (up to: Maximum number of rows to return (up to 10,000)
Geographic location of your dataset (e.g: Geographic location of your dataset (e.g. US, EU, us-central1). Leave blank for US.
Enable only if your query uses BigQuery: Enable only if your query uses BigQuery Legacy SQL syntax (not recommended)
Enter a value for each column. Fields ar: Enter a value for each column. Fields are loaded from the table schema.
An array of row objects to insert. Keys: An array of row objects to insert. Keys must match the table column names. Example: `[{"name": "Alice", "age": 30}, {"name": "Bob", "age": 25}]`
If enabled, valid rows are inserted even: If enabled, valid rows are inserted even when some rows in the batch are invalid. If disabled (default), the entire batch fails if any row is invalid.
If enabled, fields not in the table sche: If enabled, fields not in the table schema are silently discarded. If disabled (default), unknown fields cause the row to fail.
SQL condition to match the rows to delet: SQL condition to match the rows to delete. Do not include the WHERE keyword. Example: `status = "inactive" AND created_at < "2023-01-01"`
Dataset location (e.g. US, EU). Leave bl: Dataset location (e.g. US, EU). Leave blank to use the default.
Comma-separated column assignments. Do n: Comma-separated column assignments. Do not include the SET keyword. Example: `status = "active", updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP()`
SQL condition to match the rows to updat: SQL condition to match the rows to update. Do not include the WHERE keyword. Example: `id = "abc123"`
SQL condition to filter rows. Do not inc: SQL condition to filter rows. Do not include the WHERE keyword. Example: `email = "user@example.com"`
Optional column(s) to sort results befor: Optional column(s) to sort results before picking the first row. Example: `created_at DESC`
SQL condition to search for an existing: SQL condition to search for an existing row. Do not include WHERE. Example: `email = "user@example.com"`
A JSON object of column/value pairs to i: A JSON object of column/value pairs to insert if no matching row exists. Example: `{"email": "user@example.com", "name": "Alice"}`
The BigQuery job ID to fetch results for: The BigQuery job ID to fetch results for. This is returned by the "Query Job Completed" trigger or a "Run a Query" action.
Maximum number of rows to return (up to: Maximum number of rows to return (up to 10,000). Default: 1,000.
Dataset location where the job ran (e.g. US, EU). Leave blank for US.
The data to import. Provide a JSON array: The data to import. Provide a JSON array of objects (`[{...}, {...}]`) or a newline-delimited JSON string where each line is one row object.
Continue importing valid rows even if so: Continue importing valid rows even if some rows fail validation. Default: off (fail entire batch).
Silently drop fields not present in the: Silently drop fields not present in the table schema. Default: off (fail on unknown fields).
Authorization headers are injected autom: Authorization headers are injected automatically from your connection.
Enable for files like PDFs, images, etc.
HEAD: HEAD
Triggers when a new row is added to a Bi: Triggers when a new row is added to a BigQuery table. Polls every 5 minutes by comparing the latest value in a sort column against the previous check.
Triggers when an existing row is updated: Triggers when an existing row is updated in a BigQuery table. Requires an `updated_at` TIMESTAMP column that is set whenever a row changes.
Triggers when a BigQuery query job finis: Triggers when a BigQuery query job finishes successfully. Each flow run receives the job metadata and the result rows from that query.
Triggers when any BigQuery job (query, l: Triggers when any BigQuery job (query, load, copy, or extract) finishes. Returns the job metadata including status, type, and timing.
Name of the column used to detect new ro: Name of the column used to detect new rows. The table is ordered by this column (DESC) and any row newer than the last check is returned. Use a TIMESTAMP or DATETIME column, e.g. `created_at`.
Maximum number of new rows to return per: Maximum number of new rows to return per poll (default: 500).
Name of the TIMESTAMP column that record: Name of the TIMESTAMP column that records when a row was last updated, e.g. `updated_at`. Rows where this column is newer than the last check are returned.
If provided, only rows where the updated: If provided, only rows where the updated timestamp is newer than the created timestamp are returned, excluding brand-new rows from the results.
Maximum number of updated rows to return: Maximum number of updated rows to return per poll (default: 500).
Maximum number of result rows to fetch p: Maximum number of result rows to fetch per completed job (default: 500).
If enabled, jobs that completed with an: If enabled, jobs that completed with an error are also returned. Default: off (only successful jobs).
